fact: April 21 is exactly 111 days into the year - that's triple ones in a row!
In the Northern Hemisphere, dandelions are typically blooming everywhere right now, turning lawns into yellow polka-dot carpets.
April 21 sits right in that sweet spot where winter's chill is finally losing its grip but summer heat hasn't arrived yet in northern climates.
Taurus season begins around April 21 - people born on this date have the bull as their zodiac sign.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, leaves are typically turning brilliant orange and red right now as autumn kicks into high gear.
Sunrise happens about 2 minutes earlier each day around April 21 in most northern places, making mornings feel brighter.
This date falls almost exactly one month after the spring equinox, making it the middle point of early spring when plants grow rapidly.
Earthworms are usually very active right now - the soil is finally warm and moist enough for them to move around easily after winter.
April 21 often marks the time when maple trees in northern regions start dropping their helicopter seeds that spin through the air.
In northern lakes and ponds, ice-out typically happens around now - when winter ice finally cracks apart and floats away in chunks.
Cherry blossom petals are often falling like pink snow in many temperate regions, creating beautiful carpeted sidewalks.
